intro:
Unfortunately, I have to inform you that I couldn't find any information on Lathyrus montanus lusus variegatus. However, I can provide you with information on Lathyrus montanus, a plant species in the pea family (Fabaceae), and then add the variegatus part, although I couldn't verify its existence.

Lathyrus montanus, also known as the mountain pea or Montane pea, is a flowering plant that is native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce pain and improve cardiovascular health. The bioactive constituents of Lathyrus montanus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Herbal preparations made from Lathyrus montanus include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, including arthritis, digestive issues, and respiratory problems. 

If we consider the variegatus part to be a variation or a cultivar of Lathyrus montanus, then the above information would still apply, with the variegatus part possibly referring to a specific characteristic of the plant, such as its variegated or multicolored leaves.
